current.summary: Validated capmon issue processing pattern on windsurf/skills; fixed format doc accuracy issues and tightened agent instructions

activeWork.description: Processing 50 open capmon-change issues — validating and running the full remediation loop (fetch → format doc update → graduation check → derive → commit)
activeWork.progress: Ran the full syllago-capmon-process workflow manually on issue #67 (windsurf/skills) as a validation run. It worked end-to-end: format doc enriched with 4 new provider_extensions, graduation candidate found (auto_invocation_signal — windsurf+kiro, issue #73), derive ran clean, committed and pushed. Found and fixed two accuracy issues in the output: (1) user_invocable mechanism had leaked UI creation content — fixed in windsurf.yaml and update-format-doc.md now has explicit 'narrow mechanism' guidance; (2) name_format_constraint missing uniqueness requirement — fixed. 50 issues remain (10 providers × multiple content types). Only skills content type has format docs — all other content type issues will have no format doc to update (they're onboard issues for rules, hooks, commands, agents, mcp).

git.lastCommit: 8c85a5a capmon: fix windsurf format doc + tighten update-format-doc instructions

recentDecisions: Process all 50 initial onboard issues WITHOUT creating PRs — push directly to main since this is baseline establishment, not drift detection. Add branch-first step to process-issues.md AFTER the initial batch (bead syllago-7v9jq).
update-format-doc.md agent instructions now include explicit 'narrow mechanism' rule with user_invocable/ui_creation as the canonical example of the failure mode to avoid.

Onboard creates issues for ALL content types in the source manifest (not just skills). Format docs only cover skills. For non-skills content type issues (rules, hooks, commands, agents, mcp), the process-issues workflow needs to handle the case where no format doc section exists — either skip or create a stub section.
gh issue create uses URL output (not --json) — fixed in report.go. CreateCapmonChangeIssue now parses the number from the printed URL.
nextSteps: Pick a non-windsurf, non-skills issue from the 50 open and run the same full manual validation pattern: parse issue → verify cache → run format doc update agent → graduation check → derive → commit. Good candidates: amp/skills (#30 or whichever is the amp/skills issue), or claude-code/skills.

After validating a second provider/content type, formalize the processing pattern into beads: one bead per provider's skills issue (or per provider for all content types). The plan: (1) process issue, (2) validate accuracy + fix issues, (3) commit and push.

Decide how to handle the 40 non-skills issues (rules, hooks, commands, agents, mcp) — these are onboard captures with no format doc sections yet. Options: close as 'no format doc section yet' or create stub sections in the format docs.
After all 50 issues are processed, run bd sync and close syllago-jtafb.

context.notes: The capmon issue processing pattern established by the windsurf/skills run: (1) `gh issue view <N>` to parse provider/content_type from anchor; (2) verify `.capmon-cache/<slug>/<ct>-0/raw.bin` exists and hash matches issue; (3) spawn format doc update subagent with update-format-doc.md instructions; (4) verify output for accuracy — specifically watch for: mechanism field contamination (content belonging to a different key or extension bleeding in), missing constraints (uniqueness, format rules), incorrect confidence levels; (5) graduation comparison subagent if new extensions were added; (6) `syllago capmon derive --provider=<slug>`; (7) commit with 'Closes #N'; (8) push. No PRs during initial batch — push directly to main. The uncommitted files in git status are beads db and audit log — not code changes.
